# toll-booth

> L402 payment middleware for Rust. Gates any HTTP API behind Lightning payments.

toll-booth is a Rust crate that adds HTTP 402 payment gating to any API. It uses the L402 protocol (macaroons + Lightning invoices) to challenge unauthenticated requests, verify payments via preimage, and manage credit balances. Designed as a Tower Layer, it drops straight into axum.

## Key features

- Credit-balance model: one Lightning payment funds multiple requests, with automatic debit per call
- Bring-your-own backend: implement `LightningBackend` for any Lightning node (Phoenixd included)
- Bring-your-own storage: implement `StorageBackend` for any store (in-memory and SQLite included)
- Pluggable payment rails: `PaymentRail` trait supports per-request, credit, and session modes
- Free tier: configurable free requests or free credits per IP per day (IPs hashed with daily salt)
- Tiered pricing: per-route pricing with tier keys sent via `X-Toll-Tier` header
- Cost reconciliation: adjust credit balances after upstream responds with actual cost
- Custom macaroon caveats: route restrictions, IP binding, expiry, plus arbitrary key-value pairs
- axum middleware via `TollBoothLayer` (Tower Layer/Service)

## Architecture

```
Request -> TollBoothLayer -> TollBoothEngine
                                |
                                +-- Pricing table lookup
                                +-- Payment rails (L402Rail, extensible)
                                +-- Free tier fallback
                                +-- StorageBackend (credit/debit/settle)
                                +-- LightningBackend (invoice creation)
                                |
                            TollBoothResult:
                              Proxy   -> forward to handler
                              Challenge -> 402 + WWW-Authenticate
                              Pass    -> unpriced route
                              Blocked -> 403
```

## Quick start

```toml
[dependencies]
toll-booth = { version = "0.1", features = ["l402", "axum-middleware"] }
```

```rust
use std::sync::Arc;
use toll_booth::{
    TollBoothEngine, TollBoothConfig,
    L402Rail, L402RailConfig,
    MemoryStorage,
    TollBoothLayer,
    PricingEntry,
};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    let storage = Arc::new(MemoryStorage::new());

    let l402 = L402Rail::new(L402RailConfig {
        root_key: std::env::var("L402_ROOT_KEY").unwrap(),
        storage: storage.clone(),
        default_amount: 100,
        backend: None,
        service_name: Some("My API".to_string()),
    });

    let mut pricing = std::collections::HashMap::new();
    pricing.insert("/v1/chat".to_string(), PricingEntry::Simple(100));

    let engine = TollBoothEngine::new(TollBoothConfig {
        storage,
        pricing,
        upstream: "http://127.0.0.1:8080".into(),
        root_key: std::env::var("L402_ROOT_KEY").unwrap(),
        rails: vec![Box::new(l402)],
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .unwrap();

    let app = axum::Router::new()
        .route("/v1/chat", axum::routing::post(|| async { "hello" }))
        .layer(TollBoothLayer::new(engine));

    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("0.0.0.0:3000").await.unwrap();
    axum::serve(listener, app).await.unwrap();
}
```

## Public API surface

### Core types

- `TollBoothEngine` -- main engine, created from `TollBoothConfig`
- `TollBoothConfig` -- storage, pricing table, upstream URL, root key, rails, free tier
- `TollBoothResult` -- enum: `Proxy`, `Challenge`, `Pass`, `Blocked`
- `TollBoothRequest` -- method, path, headers, IP, optional tier
- `PricingEntry` -- `Simple(u64)`, `Detailed(PriceInfo)`, `Tiered(HashMap<String, u64>)`
- `FreeTierConfig` -- `Requests(u64)` or `Credits(u64)`
- `Currency` -- `Sat` or `Usd`

### Traits

- `PaymentRail` -- `detect()`, `challenge()`, `verify()` for any payment scheme
- `LightningBackend` -- `create_invoice()`, `check_invoice()`, optional `send_payment()`
- `StorageBackend` -- `credit()`, `debit()`, `balance()`, `settle()`, invoice storage, pruning

### Implementations

- `L402Rail` -- L402 payment rail (macaroon + Lightning preimage verification)
- `MemoryStorage` -- in-memory storage for tests and prototypes
- `SqliteStorage` -- SQLite storage (feature: `sqlite`)
- `PhoenixdBackend` -- Phoenixd Lightning node (feature: `phoenixd`)
- `TollBoothLayer` -- Tower Layer for axum (feature: `axum-middleware`)

### L402 protocol flow

1. Client sends request without credentials
2. Engine returns 402 with `WWW-Authenticate: L402 macaroon="...", invoice="..."`
3. Client pays Lightning invoice, receives preimage
4. Client retries with `Authorization: L402 <macaroon>:<preimage_hex>`
5. Engine verifies SHA256(preimage) == payment_hash (constant-time)
6. On first use, engine settles and credits the balance
7. Subsequent requests debit from balance until exhausted
8. Engine issues fresh 402 when balance runs out

## Feature flags

| Feature           | Default | What it enables                          |
|-------------------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| `l402`            | yes     | L402 payment rail                        |
| `axum-middleware` | yes     | Tower Layer for axum                     |
| `sqlite`          | yes     | SQLite storage backend (bundled)         |
| `phoenixd`        | no      | Phoenixd Lightning node backend          |

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/forgesworn/toll-booth-rs
